hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by improving complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 contacts while reducing off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the SSTR2 pharmacophore. However, more targeted mutagenesis is needed to ensure Δmargin > 0 across the top candidates.

proposed_changes.rationale: Disrupt conserved pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) to reduce off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions while maintaining SSTR2 binding.
